Metadata.ExomizerCommand: exomizer sfx 0x9000 "MURDERONTHEWATERFRONT-9000" -Di_irq_exit=1 -s "inc $d030 lsr $d011" -f "w1: bit $d011 bpl w1 w2: bit $d011 bmi w2 jsr $fda3 rol $d011 dec $d030" -x "inc $d020" -o "Murder on the Waterfront.prg"
Metadata.Notes:
This game has a BASIC intro program before the main game loads. However, as the main game code overwrites the intro when loaded, the following steps were taken to retain the intro:
1. A breakpoint at $C060 was set to break when the intro had loaded 2. The BASIC intro was dumped from $080D to $1D29 to skip the second section of file loading 3. The SYS 49194 instruction on line 893 was changed to SYS 37120 ($9100) instead 4. Another memory block from $C100 to $C300 was also dumped, as this contains some machine code and data needed by the intro 5. A breakpoint at $C177 was set to break when the main game had loaded 6. The modified BASIC intro was then placed at some free memory at $7001 and relinked with the BASIC subroutine at $A533 7. The memory block from $C100 to $C300 was copied to $C900 8. The memory block dumped in step 4 was restored to $C100 9. A routine was added at $9000 which sets the correct VICII and BASIC pointer values before executing the BASIC RUN command, which runs the modified intro program (now at $7001) 10. Once the intro has finished, the modified SYS call on line 893 jumps to a new routine at $9100, which restores the 512 bytes of data copied in step 7 from $C900 back to $C100, before setting the correct BASIC pointer values and executing the BASIC RUN command again to start the main game program. The routine also sets $FB, $FC, $FD and $FE to zero to avoid issues with the voice samples. 11. Lines 5 and 7 at the start of the main game program were commented out to remove additional file loading (the files are already in memory within the dump)
